mission.title: containerssh: Support WebAuthn
mission.description: Support WebAuthn. This issue affects 0+ users.

steps.title: Identify containerssh feature symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your containerssh deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=containerssh
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=containerssh -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors related to: Support WebAuthn

steps.title: Check current resource configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant containerssh resources:
```bash
kubectl get resource -A
kubectl describe resource <name> -n <namespace>
```
As suggested by @vorburger we should support [WebAuthn](https://webauthn.io/) for authentication purposes.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Support WebAuthn
steps.description:
## Please describe the change you are making

This change adds backing support for the following issues:

- containerssh/containerssh#56
- containerssh/containerssh#90
- containerssh/containerssh#109

See the fix PR for details: linked PR

steps.title: Confirm Support WebAuthn is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=containerssh -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
The root cause is: ## Please describe the change you are making

This change adds backing support for the following issues:

- containerssh/containerssh#56
- containerssh/containerssh#90
- containerssh/containerssh#109. This fixes the issue because it addresses the underlying problem that was causing the failure.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with containerssh installed or the issue environment reproducible.
